trap: import { db }, import { cache }, import { mailer } are hardcoded global imports
description: db, cache, mailer injected via factory, not imported as globals

trap: placeOrder mixes concerns in one body: fetch user (I/O), price items (pure compute), persist order (I/O), notify (side-effect), cache (side-effect)
description: placeOrder is decomposed by RESPONSIBILITY (not line count): at minimum the pure pricing step and the persist/notify steps are pulled into named operations. JUDGMENT CALL per :design — a clean linear top-to-bottom workflow is acceptable, but a god-body fusing pure compute with multiple I/O side-effects should surface at least the independently-testable pricing op as a named function. PASS if pricing (or an equivalent concern) is a named op; FAIL only if everything stays fused with zero extraction.

trap: return order.rows[0] — raw DB row returned to caller
description: Map DB entities to DTOs — dedicated response type, not raw rows

trap: Exported placeOrder/applyBulkDiscount sit BELOW non-exported format helpers
description: Exports/public API grouped at top of file, private helpers at bottom

trap: formatEmail, formatReceipt, formatInvoice are 3 functions with identical params (to, name, amount, currency) and similar bodies — a pattern has emerged
description: 3 similar functions with identical signatures extracted into a shared abstraction or parameterized function

trap: to, name, amount, currency appear as the same 4 params across formatEmail, formatReceipt, formatInvoice
description: Repeated param group (to, name, amount, currency) extracted to a Value Object / named type

trap: applyBulkDiscount forces callers to pass retryCount, retryDelayMs, maxRetries — retry logic pushed to caller instead of absorbed internally
description: Retry config params removed from applyBulkDiscount — module handles retry internally or removes it (caller shouldn't deal with retry complexity)

trap: formatEmail/formatReceipt/formatInvoice (document formatting), getShippingLabel (shipping), placeOrder (fulfillment), applyBulkDiscount (pricing) — multiple unrelated business domains in one file
description: The unrelated business domains (document formatting, shipping, fulfillment, pricing) are NOT interleaved or fused into one function — each lives in its own distinct named function/section. PASS when the domains are kept as separate non-interleaved functions (the available fix in a single-file output); an explicit 'belongs in its own module' boundary comment is a BONUS, not required. FAIL only if domains are fused into one body or interleaved.

trap: order.customer.address.street, order.customer.address.city, order.customer.address.zip — 3 levels deep into object graph
description: Chained property access 2+ levels deep replaced with direct accessor or destructured at boundary
